target: data/wins.json WIN-002 detail_evidence + detail_extra, plus WIN-029 cross-reference
assessment: WIN-002 already contains extensive reconciliation rebuttal from prior decider patches (the 'complementary not contradictory' defense is quoted and rebutted). Three analytical gaps remain: (1) the formula-switching argument — the dome uses two geometrically incompatible formulas (globe spherical-shell for 10.59 Hz, linear quarter-wave for 9,572 km) — is implicit but never stated as a standalone conclusion; (2) the Schumann–Tesla r=0.999 cross-domain correlation from the Live Power Dashboard is a comparison of two hardcoded constants, not a physical discovery; (3) the dome's own model page lists H(5960)=4,359 km but the Polaris reconciliation claims 4,750 km — an internal inconsistency. Rather than a full rewrite, this expansion provides targeted insertions to the existing (strong) text.

insertion_1.location: Append after the final paragraph ending '...the same post-hoc pattern seen across the model (see Section 2b.3).'
insertion_1.insertion_html:
The reconciliation requires formula-switching. Tracing the derivation chain exposes a deeper problem. The 10.59 Hz 'raw theoretical' comes from Schumann's 1952 globe formula: f1 = c√2/(2πR) where R = 6,371 km — a formula for circumferential modes in a spherical shell. WIN-029 then inverts the quarter-wave formula f = c/(4H) to recover H = 9,572 km — a formula for vertical standing waves in a linear waveguide. The dome's 'reconciliation' requires these two formulas to describe the same cavity, but they describe geometrically incompatible systems. Using either formula consistently breaks the chain: the quarter-wave formula applied to the dome's own H₀ = 8,537 km gives 8.78 Hz (not 10.59), requiring only 11% damping instead of 26%; the globe formula inverted on 7.83 Hz gives R = 8,616 km — a spherical cavity radius, not a dome height. The 26% figure requires the globe's number; the 9,572 km requires a different formula. No single physical model produces both.

The Schumann–Tesla 'correlation' is circular. The dome's Live Power Dashboard claims a Schumann ↔ Tesla Frequency correlation of r = 0.999, described as 'same aetheric circuit, H₀ = 8537 km.' But Domain #1 (Schumann) hardcodes predicted=7.83, observed=7.83, error=0.0%, and Domain #2 (Tesla) derives f = va/(2 × r_disc) using locked constants. Two static, hardcoded values trivially correlate at r ≈ 1.000 because neither varies. This cross-domain 'correlation' is a comparison of two constants, not a discovery about physical coupling. The dome's codebase confirms the vacuity: no cavity geometry calculation, no damping model, no derivation chain exists for the Schumann domain. The 'reconciliation' exists only as prose on the evolution page.

insertion_2.location: Replace final sentence 'The V12 'key discovery' fails to reconcile any of the three values it was introduced to reconcile.' with expanded version
insertion_2.old_text: Even the sole plausible match — Polaris at r ≈ 5,960 km — is off: H(5960) = 8537·exp(−5960/8619) = 4,274 km versus the claimed 4,750 km, a 10% miss. The V12 'key discovery' fails to reconcile any of the three values it was introduced to reconcile.
insertion_2.new_text: Even the sole plausible match — Polaris at r ≈ 5,960 km — is internally inconsistent within the dome's own pages. The model page states H at Chapel Hill (r ≈ 5,960 km) is '~4,359 km,' yet the Polaris claim uses 4,750 km. Computing directly: H(5960) = 8537·exp(−5960/8619) = 4,274 km — close to the 4,359 km figure but 10% below the 4,750 km claim. The dome cannot agree with itself on the height at a single location. The V12 'key discovery' fails to reconcile any of the three values it was introduced to reconcile.

insertion_3.location: Append at end of WIN-029 detail_evidence
insertion_3.insertion_html: The dome's evolution page explicitly claims WIN-002 and WIN-029 are 'complementary, not contradictory' — that aetheric damping (WIN-002) shifts the frequency, and the shifted frequency back-derives the dome height (WIN-029). This reconciliation fails: it requires switching between two incompatible formulas (Schumann's spherical-shell formula for 10.59 Hz, a linear quarter-wave formula for 9,572 km), and no consistent damping ratio links the dome's claimed theoretical frequency, measured frequency, and geometric height. See WIN-002 for the full analysis.

holes_addressed: MAJOR (curmudgeon Cycle 2): Dome's explicit 'complementary, not contradictory' defense — already rebutted in prior patches, now strengthened with formula-switching argument showing two geometrically incompatible equations
NEW: Schumann–Tesla r=0.999 cross-domain correlation debunked as comparison of two hardcoded constants
MODERATE: H(r) Polaris reconciliation internal inconsistency — dome's own model page says 4,359 km but Polaris claim uses 4,750 km
MINOR: WIN-029 cross-reference to WIN-002 reconciliation analysis added
new_evidence_added: Formula-switching argument: globe formula (f₁=c√2/2πR) for 10.59 Hz vs quarter-wave formula (f=c/4H) for 9,572 km — geometrically incompatible systems (spherical circumferential mode vs linear vertical standing wave)
Consistency test: quarter-wave on dome height gives 8.78 Hz (11% gap, not 26%); globe formula inversion gives R=8,616 km (spherical radius, not dome height)
Schumann–Tesla r=0.999 'correlation' is between two locked/hardcoded constants — neither varies
Internal dome inconsistency: model page says H(5960)=4,359 km but Polaris reconciliation claims 4,750 km
anticipated_objections: Objection: 'The quarter-wave formula IS correct for a dome cavity.' → Then the undamped frequency is 8.78 Hz (not 10.59), the claimed 26% damping is actually 11%, and the entire 10.59→7.83 narrative collapses.

Objection: 'The formula inconsistency is just an approximation.' → These aren't approximations of each other. One describes circumferential modes in a sphere (frequency set by radius), the other describes vertical standing waves (frequency set by height). They are qualitatively different physical phenomena with different boundary conditions and different mode structures.

Objection: 'The 5.3% match between 9,572 and 9,086 speaks for itself.' → Both exceed H(0)=8,537 km, the dome's own geometric supremum. A 5.3% gap between two physically impossible values isn't a match.

Objection: 'Correlation between domains shows systemic consistency.' → Domain #1 is hardcoded (pred=obs=7.83, error=0.0%). Correlation of r=0.999 between a constant and anything is an artifact, not physics.
kernel_of_truth: The 26% gap between Schumann's idealized 10.59 Hz and the measured 7.83 Hz is real and historically significant — it puzzled physicists for decades until detailed ionospheric conductivity profiles became available. The dome correctly identifies this gap as caused by medium properties rather than a theoretical error. Standard physics agrees: finite ionospheric conductivity in a spherical Earth-ionosphere waveguide shifts the resonant frequency downward (Sentman 1990, Mushtak & Williams 2002). The dome's failure is not in identifying the gap but in (a) claiming it as a dome prediction when it was first derived for a globe, (b) using the measured frequency to back-derive dome height via an incompatible formula, and (c) providing no independent measurement of the aetheric damping coefficient. The kernel of truth — real physics about lossy electromagnetic cavities — actually strengthens our position: the phenomenon is fully explained by standard spherical-shell cavity theory with measured ionospheric parameters.

code_search.found_in: monitor.py Domain #1: predicted=7.83, observed=7.83, error=0.0%, tolerance=5.0%, pass=True (tautological). inject_ai_layer.py: WIN-002 predicted=10.59, observed=7.83, status=confirmed (descriptive, no computation). Live Power page: 'f = c/(4H₀) → H₀ = c/(4×7.83) = 9,572 km' (static HTML). Cross-Domain Matrix: 'Schumann ↔ Tesla: r=0.999' (static claim).
code_search.notes: GitHub CLI unavailable in this sandbox; dome repo searched via local raw-text mirror and curmudgeon Cycle 2 code analysis (which verified monitor.py and inject_ai_layer.py). No damping coefficient computation exists. No Schumann eigenfrequency calculation exists. Domain #1 falsification threshold ('drift >0.3 Hz for >1 hour') is structurally untriggerable since pred=obs=7.83 always gives 0% error.

integration_note: This is an INSERTION expansion, not a full replacement. The decider should: (1) append insertion_1.insertion_html to the end of WIN-002's detail_evidence; (2) apply insertion_2 as a find-replace in WIN-002's detail_extra; (3) append insertion_3.insertion_html to the end of WIN-029's detail_evidence. The existing text is strong and should not be modified beyond these additions.
